commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the grouped chat delete confirmation popover to fixed viewport placement with clamped coordinates and adds jsdom geometry tests for top-edge, bottom-edge, and narrow-width cases.

Reproducibility: no. not by a high-confidence live run. Source inspection shows current main positions the popover absolutely inside the scrollable chat area, which makes the reported clipping source-reproducible but not browser-reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof is the merge blocker; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(ui): keep delete confirm in viewport
target.body:
## Summary

- Position the chat delete confirmation popover relative to the viewport instead of the scroll container.
- Choose above or below the delete button based on available vertical space.
- Clamp the popover horizontally so it remains reachable on narrow viewports.
- Add focused jsdom geometry coverage for top-edge, bottom-edge, and narrow-width placement.

## User-visible behavior

The delete confirmation popover in Control UI chat should remain visible and reachable when deleting messages near the top or edges of the chat viewport.

## Real behavior proof

Environment: local checkout on Raspberry Pi-class host, Node v22.21.1, branch `fix-chat-delete-confirm-viewport` at `fb9b734d71`.

Exact local validation run after the patch:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
```

Observed result: command exited 0 with no whitespace errors.

Additional source-level verification:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` now measures the delete button and popover, selects `above` or `below`, and writes clamped fixed-position `top`/`left` styles.
- `ui/src/styles/chat/grouped.css` now uses `position: fixed` for `.chat-delete-confirm`, so the popover is no longer clipped by the scrollable chat container.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` includes focused geometry tests for top-edge placement, bottom-edge placement, and horizontal clamping.

What was not tested locally: I did not install dependencies or run the Vitest suite on this Raspberry Pi host to avoid heavy local work. CI should run the focused and wider lanes.

## AI-assisted

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff and understand the change scope.
